name: @radzor/error-tracker
description: Capture and report errors to external services (Sentry-style). Supports exceptions, messages, context enrichment, and breadcrumb trails. Uses raw fetch to a configurable endpoint — zero dependencies.

inputs.name: endpoint
inputs.description: HTTP endpoint to POST error reports to.

inputs.name: dsn
inputs.description: Optional DSN string included in the report header for service identification.

inputs.name: maxBreadcrumbs
inputs.description: Maximum number of breadcrumbs to retain per report.

inputs.name: headers
inputs.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with each report.

outputs.name: errorReport
outputs.description: Structured error report sent to the endpoint.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Additional context attached to the report.

fields.name: breadcrumbs
fields.description: Trail of breadcrumbs leading up to the error.

events.name: onErrorCaptured
events.description: Fired when an error or message is captured and reported.

actions.name: captureException

params.name: extra
params.description: Additional context for this specific error.

actions.description: Capture an exception and send it to the endpoint. Returns the report ID.

actions.name: captureMessage
actions.description: Capture a message (not an exception) and send it. Returns the report ID.

actions.name: setContext
actions.description: Set persistent context that is attached to all subsequent reports.

actions.name: addBreadcrumb

params.name: breadcrumb
params.type: { category: string; message: string; level?: string; data?: Record<string, unknown> }
params.description: Breadcrumb to add to the trail.

actions.description: Add a breadcrumb to the current trail. Oldest breadcrumbs are evicted when maxBreadcrumbs is reached.

connectsTo.description: Route captured errors to notification channels

llm.constraints: Uses raw fetch() to POST JSON error reports — the endpoint must accept JSON. Breadcrumbs are stored in memory and capped at maxBreadcrumbs. Context is cumulative (setContext merges). Reports are fire-and-forget — network failures are silently ignored to avoid recursive error loops.
